prompt:
This folder contains the full source of the Python-Markdown library (importable as `markdown` from this directory). BUG REPORT: a shortcut reference link whose label contains inline code is not converted to a link. Reproduction: import markdown markdown.markdown("[`Text`]\n\n[`Text`]: http://example.com") returns '

[Text]

' but must return '

Text

'. The same failure occurs when the code span is only part of the label, e.g. [some `Text`] or [`Text` after] with matching reference definitions. Find the root cause and fix it in the library source. Requirements: the reported cases must work, and ALL other behavior must stay exactly the same - inline links containing code spans, plain code spans (including brackets inside code and double-backtick spans), plain and two-part reference links must keep rendering as before. Note: pytest is NOT installed in this environment, so do not try to run the bundled test suite; verify your fix by running small python snippets with markdown.markdown() directly. Your fix will be graded by hidden tests covering the reported cases plus regression checks on surrounding behavior.
